title: k3s Cluster Stability & Gold Standard Audit
scope: receptor-infra — k3s cluster configuration, Helm values, RBAC, network policies, monitoring, backup strategy, provisioning, and documentation

findings.description: No `--secrets-encryption` flag on k3s servers. Kubernetes Secrets are stored base64-encoded in etcd without AES-256 at-rest encryption. Anyone with etcd access or a database snapshot can decode all cluster secrets.

tasks.description: Enable secrets encryption on all three control-plane nodes by adding `--secrets-encryption` to the k3s server systemd service args (/etc/rancher/k3s/config.yaml or ExecStart). Requires rolling restart of each control-plane node in sequence.
Document the secrets-encryption key rotation procedure in `docs/operations/secrets-encryption.md` and add to the provisioning README.

findings.description: No etcd snapshot schedule configured (`--etcd-snapshot-schedule-cron`, `--etcd-snapshot-s3-*`). ADR-004 covers only Postgres pg_dump. A control-plane node loss or etcd corruption requires a full cluster rebuild with no point-in-time restore.

tasks.description: Add `--etcd-snapshot-schedule-cron='0 */6 * * *'` and `--etcd-snapshot-retention=14` to k3s config on all control-plane nodes.
Configure external snapshot storage pointing to the existing Azure Blob `k3sbackups71a475f1dae6` container (australiaeast, satisfying APP 8).
Extend ADR-004 to document etcd snapshot strategy and add etcd restore procedure to `docs/operations/incident-response.md`.
Add a Prometheus alert for etcd snapshot staleness (alert if last snapshot > 8 hours old) in `monitoring/prometheus-rules.yaml`.

findings.description: Vault is deployed in standalone mode (`standalone.enabled: true`) pinned to a single node (`receptor-ctrl-50`). If this node or pod becomes unavailable, all VSO secret syncs fail, blocking ARC runner auth, Alertmanager webhook delivery, Zot GHCR sync, and Longhorn backup credentials.

tasks.description: Evaluate Vault HA mode with integrated Raft storage across 3 Vault pods, one per control-plane node. Document decision in a new or updated ADR. If HA is deferred, add formal exception to ADR-006 with a PodDisruptionBudget and recovery runbook.
Add a critical Prometheus alert: `absent(kube_pod_status_ready{condition='true', namespace='vault'})` for >2 minutes, in `monitoring/prometheus-rules.yaml`.

findings.description: Falco runtime security chart is commented out in helmfile.yaml. Custom rules exist in `falco/falco-rules.yaml` indicating prior intent. No process currently detects privilege escalation, shell spawning, or container escapes. The `node-maintenance` DaemonSet runs with `privileged: true` and `hostPID: true`.

tasks.description: Diagnose why Falco was disabled: check git log for the commit that commented it out and any referenced issue.
Re-enable the Falco Helm release in helmfile.yaml with the existing values/falco.yaml. Add falcosecurity repo to the repositories section.
Create ADR-012 for Falco: document deployment decision, custom rules rationale, and exception handling for privileged DaemonSets (node-maintenance).

findings.description: `provisioning/user-data` join commands are incomplete: missing `--cluster-init` for the first control-plane and correct `--server` flag for subsequent nodes. The embedded etcd bootstrap mode is not codified.

tasks.description: Update the k3s join comment in `provisioning/user-data` to show `--cluster-init` for the first node and `--server https://10.10.0.10:6443` for additional control-plane nodes.

findings.description: No k3s config file (`/etc/rancher/k3s/config.yaml`) is committed to the repository. The `--flannel-backend=none`, `--disable-network-policy`, `--node-ip` Calico flags are in inline comments only. The cluster cannot be reproduced without tribal knowledge.

tasks.description: Commit k3s config templates at `provisioning/k3s-server-config.yaml.template` and `provisioning/k3s-agent-config.yaml.template` including all flags: `--flannel-backend=none`, `--disable-network-policy`, `--node-ip`, `--cluster-init`, `--secrets-encryption`.

findings.description: No k3s API server audit logging configured. `docs/security/audit-logging.md` covers only pgaudit (Postgres), not the Kubernetes API server audit trail. Gap against ISO 27001 A.8.15 and CIS Kubernetes Benchmark 3.2.

tasks.description: Create `provisioning/audit-policy.yaml` and add `--kube-apiserver-arg=audit-policy-file=...` and `--kube-apiserver-arg=audit-log-path=...` to k3s server config. Start with Metadata level for all requests.
Configure Promtail (Loki) to scrape k3s API audit logs from control-plane nodes and make them queryable in Grafana.

findings.description: Vault data and audit storage uses `storageClass: local-path`, not Longhorn. Local-path PVs have no replication and are bound to `receptor-ctrl-50`. A disk failure results in permanent Vault data loss.

tasks.description: Migrate Vault data and audit PVCs to `storageClassName: longhorn` with 3-replica replication. Requires a Vault seal → data migration → re-unseal sequence. Document in `docs/operations/vault-pvc-migration.md`.

findings.description: `rbac/namespaces.yaml` has no `pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce` labels. Kubernetes Pod Security Standards (PSS) are not enforced. Privileged workloads can be deployed without system-level gating.

tasks.description: Add PSS labels to all namespaces in `rbac/namespaces.yaml`. Use `baseline` for application namespaces; explicitly grant `privileged` to `kube-system`, `longhorn-system`, `arc-runners` with documented rationale.

findings.description: Monitoring namespace egress is `egress: [{}]` (fully open). Inline comment claims Calico GlobalNetworkPolicy restricts monitoring writes to supabase/vault data paths, but no GlobalNetworkPolicy resource exists in the repository — providing false assurance.

tasks.description: Replace the catch-all `egress: [{}]` with explicit rules: DNS (53), pod CIDR scrape ports, Alertmanager webhook (HTTPS 443). Remove the false-assurance comment about GlobalNetworkPolicy.
Either create the Calico GlobalNetworkPolicy that the comment claims exists, or remove the comment. If creating it, add to `network-policies/` and reference it in ADR-005.

findings.description: Zot registry is a single replica (`replicaCount: 1`) on `local-path` storage. A node failure invalidates all CI runner image caches, causing Docker Hub rate-limit exposure during cache rebuild.

tasks.description: Evaluate: (a) increase to 2 replicas with Longhorn RWX storage, or (b) move to purely on-demand pull-through mode with no local persistence. Document decision in ADR or helmfile comment.

findings.description: The k3s join URL is hardcoded to `receptor-ctrl-10` (10.10.0.10). If ctrl-10 is unavailable, new scheduling and kubectl operations fail even though ctrl-11 and ctrl-50 are healthy etcd members.

tasks.description: Add a Cloudflare Tunnel TCP route or HAProxy load balancer distributing port 6443 across all three control-plane IPs. Update provisioning docs and kubectl access documentation to reference the load-balanced endpoint.

findings.description: Vault has `global.tlsDisable: true`. Pod-to-VSO and pod-to-client traffic is plain HTTP. A Calico policy misconfiguration or lateral movement exposes Vault tokens in transit.

tasks.description: Enable Vault TLS using a cert-manager Certificate for the `vault.vault.svc` hostname. Update VSO VaultAuth resources to use the HTTPS endpoint. Document in ADR-003.

findings.description: Prometheus Operator admission webhooks disabled (`admissionWebhooks.enabled: false`) due to upgrade timeouts. Malformed PrometheusRule CRs are silently accepted — a broken alert rule simply does not fire.

tasks.description: Investigate the upgrade timeout root cause. Try increasing `admissionWebhooks.timeoutSeconds`. Re-enable webhooks once the timeout issue is resolved.

findings.description: Longhorn UI ingress uses plain HTTP entrypoint (`web`, port 80). Storage management operations are unencrypted between Cloudflare edge and Traefik.

tasks.description: Switch Longhorn UI ingress annotation to `websecure` entrypoint and add TLS configuration.

findings.description: `docs/security/audit-logging.md` (line 160) references Cloudflare R2 APAC as the 90-day pgaudit log archive destination. ADR-004 documents that R2 APAC was replaced by Azure Blob `australiaeast` for AU data residency compliance. The doc references a superseded backend.

tasks.description: Update `docs/security/audit-logging.md` log retention table to reference Azure Blob `k3sbackups71a475f1dae6` (australiaeast), consistent with ADR-004.

findings.description: The descheduler was designed to re-balance pods after ENTERPRISE node recovery using `RemovePodsViolatingNodeAffinity`. Node-wide affinities have since been removed, making the affinity plugin obsolete. The descheduler CronJob now runs `LowNodeUtilization` every 5 minutes, which may cause unnecessary pod churn on workloads without PodDisruptionBudgets.

tasks.description: Review whether LowNodeUtilization at 5-minute frequency is still desired. If ENTERPRISE stability is confirmed long-term, increase schedule to every 30 minutes or disable the descheduler. Update DeschedulerPolicy comments to reflect current intent.

findings.description: `AppPodCrashLooping` fires at >3 restarts in 15 minutes only. This misses persistent low-rate crash loops (1–2 restarts per hour) that can degrade services over days.

tasks.description: Add a complementary slow crash-loop alert: `rate(kube_pod_container_status_restarts_total[1h]) > 0.5` with 30-minute `for` duration and `warning` severity.

findings.description: The k3s join commands are inline comments without a reproducible installation method. Emergency node replacement requires tribal knowledge of the correct `--flannel-backend=none` and `--disable-network-policy` flags.

tasks.description: Create `provisioning/README.md` documenting the exact k3s install command sequence (including all required flags) for both control-plane and worker nodes.

findings.description: `rbac/namespaces.yaml` pre-declares the `falco` namespace but no ADR explains why Falco was disabled, what mitigations exist, or when re-enablement is expected.

tasks.description: Create `docs/adr/ADR-012-runtime-security-falco.md` explaining the disable decision and timeline for re-enablement, or remove the `falco` namespace declaration if Falco is permanently decommissioned.

findings.description: `values/vault.yaml` header reads `# Helm values for hashicorp/vault (v0.28.1)` but `helmfile.yaml` uses `version: '0.32.0'` (upgraded 2026-03-15). Stale comment.

tasks.description: Update the comment at line 2 of `values/vault.yaml` to read `v0.32.0`.

findings.description: policies/namespace-isolation.yaml declares a ClusterPolicy (kyverno.io/v1) to restrict CI runner namespace scope, but Kyverno is NOT installed — it is absent from helmfile.yaml. The policy is an orphaned resource that silently has no effect. The CI runner namespace restriction it was designed to enforce is not active, meaning sa-github-runner is not constrained to reactor-ci-* namespaces as intended.

tasks.description: Decide: (a) add Kyverno to helmfile.yaml to activate the existing ClusterPolicy, or (b) replace with a RBAC-based control (ValidatingWebhookConfiguration or namespace-level ResourceQuota), or (c) delete the orphaned policy and accept the gap. Document decision in ADR or helmfile comment.

If Kyverno is chosen (option a): add the kyverno helm chart to helmfile.yaml with appropriate resource limits and a dedicated kyverno namespace. Verify the ClusterPolicy is active with kubectl get clusterpolicy.

findings.description: No Prometheus alert monitors etcd database size. etcd has a default 2GB write quota; when the DB hits the limit, all writes to the cluster state are silently rejected (MVCC error). monitoring/prometheus-rules.yaml has no alert for etcd_mvcc_db_total_size_in_bytes approaching the quota, nor for etcd cluster health degradation.

tasks.description: Add a Prometheus alert to monitoring/prometheus-rules.yaml: fire warning at >75% of etcd 2GB quota (etcd_mvcc_db_total_size_in_bytes > 1.5e9) and critical at >90% (> 1.8e9). Also alert if etcd_server_health_failures > 0 for >2 minutes.
Add etcd defragmentation to the node-maintenance DaemonSet schedule (or a separate CronJob) to reclaim fragmented DB space. Run defrag against each etcd member sequentially, not concurrently.

findings.description: The arc-runner-receptor-infra runner set uses serviceAccountName: helmfile-deployer (cluster-admin ClusterRoleBinding) for ALL receptor-infra CI jobs. While helmfile.yaml documents this is intentional (GitOps sync requires cluster-admin), the runner set should be split: a restricted runner for lint/test jobs and a dedicated helmfile-sync runner with cluster-admin. This limits blast radius if a CI script in a non-sync job is compromised. The helmfile-deployer SA should only be bound to the sync runner.

tasks.description: Create a second ARC runner set arc-runner-receptor-infra-sync with serviceAccountName: helmfile-deployer (cluster-admin) and maxRunners: 1. This runner handles only the helmfile sync workflow.

Remove serviceAccountName from the existing arc-runner-receptor-infra runner set (or set it to a read-only SA). Update the cluster-sync GitHub workflow to use runs-on: [self-hosted, receptor-infra-sync] so only the sync runner uses cluster-admin.
Create a minimal ClusterRole for the non-sync runner SA (read pods/nodes/services only) and bind it. Document the two-runner separation in README.md.

findings.description: monitoring/prometheus-rules.yaml has no alert for Loki storage exhaustion. Loki's monitoring namespace PVC is not monitored for approaching capacity. If Loki's disk fills up, log ingestion silently stops — the cluster loses observability at the exact moment it is most needed (during an incident). No alert for loki_ingester_wal_disk_full or kubelet_volume_stats_used_bytes approaching limits.

tasks.description: Add PVC capacity alerts to monitoring/prometheus-rules.yaml: warn when any PVC in the monitoring namespace exceeds 80% capacity (kubelet_volume_stats_used_bytes / kubelet_volume_stats_capacity_bytes > 0.8). Use a namespace selector to cover Loki, Prometheus, and Grafana PVCs.

findings.description: git history shows DR-56 raised cert-manager cainjector's resource limits from 100m/128Mi to 500m/256Mi after OOM crash loops. This reactive fix pattern suggests no proactive resource sizing review has been done for other cert-manager controllers (controller, webhook). Nor is there a Prometheus alert for cert-manager component OOM events. The same brittle pattern may recur on the next upgrade.

tasks.description: Review resource requests/limits for cert-manager controller and webhook pods in values/cert-manager.yaml (or equivalent). Benchmark actual usage via kubectl top pods -n cert-manager and set limits to 150% of observed P99. Add a Prometheus alert for OOMKilled events in the cert-manager namespace.

findings.description: values/kyverno.yaml exists with full Prometheus metrics config and resource allocation, referenced in prior audit 260316-terraform-iac-gap RBAC-01-T1. Kyverno was tracked as planned infrastructure then removed from helmfile.yaml — a regression. Both values/kyverno.yaml and policies/namespace-isolation.yaml are dead weight without the chart deployed.

tasks.description: Re-add the Kyverno Helm release to helmfile.yaml using the existing values/kyverno.yaml. The chart was referenced in audit 260316-terraform-iac-gap RBAC-01-T1. Add kyverno Helm repo and release ensuring it deploys before the ClusterPolicy in sync order.

Verify the ClusterPolicy in policies/namespace-isolation.yaml activates after Kyverno is deployed. Test with: kubectl get clusterpolicy and attempt to create a namespace violating the policy as a non-admin user.

findings.description: docs/runbooks/ directory does not exist. No Prometheus alert in monitoring/prometheus-rules.yaml includes a runbook_url annotation. 12+ alerts exist covering crash-looping, Longhorn degradation, ARC runner health, and node pressure — each requires operator action but provides no linked procedure for triage and resolution.

tasks.description: Create docs/runbooks/ directory with runbooks for each Critical and High alert: CrashLooping.md, LonghornDegraded.md, VaultUnavailable.md, NodeDiskPressure.md, ARCRunnerFleetDown.md. Each runbook must include: symptoms, triage steps, resolution steps, escalation path.

Add runbook_url annotations to each alert in monitoring/prometheus-rules.yaml pointing to the corresponding runbook file in GitHub: https://github.com/Common-Bond/receptor-infra/blob/main/docs/runbooks/AlertName.md

findings.description: values/loki.yaml line 14 comment states: 'archive to R2 monthly by external cron'. No such CronJob or external cron exists anywhere in the repository. R2 APAC was superseded by Azure Blob per ADR-004. The 30-day online Loki PVC is the only log retention tier — there is no archive path and the comment references a non-existent process to a deprecated storage backend.

tasks.description: Remove the comment 'archive to R2 monthly by external cron' from values/loki.yaml since no such cron exists and R2 was superseded by Azure Blob (ADR-004). Either create a Kubernetes CronJob to export logs to Azure Blob Storage australiaeast or document that 30-day online Loki retention is the only tier.

If an archive tier is desired: create a CronJob using logcli to export Loki data to Azure Blob Storage and update the retention table in docs/security/audit-logging.md to reflect the actual archive tier and correct storage destination.

findings.description: All Helm chart versions in helmfile.yaml are manually pinned with no automated update process. No Renovate configuration, no Dependabot, and no .github/workflows/ directory exists in receptor-infra. The helmfile.yaml header documents the manual upgrade process. Security patches and critical bug fixes in Calico, Traefik, cert-manager, Vault, and kube-prometheus-stack only land when noticed manually.

tasks.description: Add a Renovate configuration file (renovate.json) to receptor-infra to track helm chart versions in helmfile.yaml. Configure minor/patch updates to auto-PR and major bumps to require manual approval.

As an alternative: add a weekly GitHub Actions workflow that runs helm repo update and checks for outdated chart versions, then opens a summary issue. Evaluate the GitHub App install requirement before choosing Renovate vs. custom workflow.

findings.description: values/loki.yaml sets Loki memory requests and limits to identical values: 256Mi requests, 256Mi limits. Loki spikes memory during high-cardinality log ingestion bursts (e.g. incident events with many distinct label combinations). With requests == limits, any spike triggers an immediate OOM kill, silently dropping logs at the worst possible moment. Gold standard is limits >= 2x requests.

tasks.description: In values/loki.yaml, set Loki memory limit to 512Mi (2x current 256Mi) while keeping requests at 256Mi. This gives headroom for cardinality spikes without increasing baseline resource cost. Monitor actual usage via Prometheus for 2 weeks post-change.

clarifications.decision: Pending human decision. Vault HA (3 pods, integrated Raft) is the gold standard but requires significant migration effort. If chosen, it eliminates VAULT-01 and VAULT-02. If deferred, document as a formal exception in ADR-006 with a PodDisruptionBudget and recovery runbook.

Pending. Enabling --secrets-encryption on an existing cluster requires a rolling restart and re-encryption of all existing secrets. Schedule during a maintenance window. k3s docs warn this can corrupt the cluster if steps are not followed in order.
Pending investigation. Git history must be checked to identify the commit that disabled Falco and any referenced issue before re-enablement is planned.

implementationPhases.name: Critical Security — Secrets Encryption and Runtime Security
implementationPhases.rationale: Secrets encryption and runtime security (Falco) are the highest-leverage security improvements and address the root cause of cluster brittleness.

implementationPhases.name: Data Durability — etcd Snapshots and Vault Storage
implementationPhases.rationale: Without etcd snapshots and Vault on replicated storage, a single node disk failure is a cluster-ending event. These must be addressed before any further work.

implementationPhases.name: Security Hardening — API Audit, PSS, Network Policies, Vault TLS
implementationPhases.rationale: Completes the security hardening layer needed to meet CIS benchmark and ISO 27001 controls.

implementationPhases.name: Resilience — Zot HA, API Load Balancer, Provisioning Reproducibility
implementationPhases.rationale: Improves cluster resilience and eliminates tribal knowledge from the emergency recovery path.

implementationPhases.name: Tech Debt and Documentation
implementationPhases.rationale: Cleans up tech debt, documentation drift, and purpose drift findings that do not carry immediate operational risk.
